meta.page_title: Free Preschool Karate Class — Ages 3–5 · A First Class for First-Timers
meta.meta_description: A free 30-minute intro karate class built for ages 3–5. Play-based, gentle, and parents stay in the room. Too young? That's exactly who this class is for.

final_cta.headline: Ready to save your child's spot?
final_cta.subheadline: Free · 30 minutes · parents stay and watch

thank_you.body: Check your email — we'll confirm your class time shortly.

testimonials.eyebrow: From parents who were unsure too
testimonials.headline: "I thought she was too young…"

items.quote: "I honestly thought she was too young and would just cry. She was quiet the first week — and now she picks out her gi the night before."
items.name: Gabriela M.

items.quote: "He cried the entire first class and clung to my leg. The instructor said 'that's totally normal, come back next week.' Now he runs in."
items.name: Danny K.

items.quote: "It's his first ever activity. I didn't expect a 3-year-old to follow along — but it's all games, so he doesn't even realize he's learning."
items.name: Renée A.

items.a: Almost certainly not. The class is built for ages 3–5, and readiness is what it builds — not something your child needs to bring. If they can walk and follow a simple game, they can start.

items.q: What if they cry or have a meltdown?
items.a: Completely normal, and completely okay. You're right there in the room. If they need to sit with you and watch, that's a fine first class — and you're welcome back next week, free, to try again.
Yes. For this age, parents stay in the room and watch the whole time. No drop-off, no separation — you're part of the first class.
Comfy clothes they can move in — a t-shirt and stretchy pants are perfect. Bare feet or grippy socks. Nothing to buy for the free class.

items.q: Do they need to be potty trained?
items.a: TODO: confirm with the school. (Common answer: potty training isn't required for the free class since a parent stays in the room, but check the school's policy before launch.)

items.q: What happens after the free class?
items.a: No pressure, no hard sell. If your child loved it, we'll walk you through the preschool program and schedule. If it wasn't the right fit yet, that's genuinely fine — many families come back a few months later.

reframe.headline: Readiness isn't required. It's what the class builds.
reframe.body: Your child doesn't need to sit still, take turns, or follow directions yet. Those are the exact skills a first class starts to grow — gently, through play.

items.title: Listening
items.description: Tuning in to an instructor's voice in a room full of other kids.

items.title: Taking turns
items.description: Waiting for a turn, then cheering on the friend who goes next.

items.title: Following directions
items.description: "Hands up, feet together" — following two-step directions, one game at a time.

hero.headline: test Too young for karate? [[That's exactly who this class is for.]]
hero.subheadline: A free 30-minute intro class for ages 3–5. Games, giggles, and their first "yes ma'am." Parents stay and watch.

image.alt: A laughing preschool girl in an oversized white karate gi mid-class, with other children and watching parents behind her

form.heading: Save your child's spot

form.disclaimer: 60-second signup · no card · parents stay and watch

instructor.bio: TODO: one warm paragraph — years teaching, why they love this age, and a human detail. Keep it about the kids, not the trophies.

photo.alt: A smiling black-belt karate instructor sitting cross-legged on the dojo mat, holding a sheet of star stickers

how_it_works.headline: What the class actually looks like

image.alt: A black-belt instructor kneeling to high-five a smiling preschool girl in a white gi, with parents watching from a bench behind them

steps.title: Circle-up game
steps.body: Everyone sits in a circle for a name game — a soft landing so no one feels put on the spot.

steps.title: Animal-movement drill
steps.body: Bear crawls, crab walks, kangaroo hops — the wiggles come out and the giggles start.

steps.title: One karate move
steps.body: Just one — a punch or a block — taught slow, celebrated big. Their first real "yes ma'am."

steps.title: High-five graduation
steps.body: A line of high-fives and a sticker. They leave feeling like they did something big — because they did.

how_it_works.note: The instructor is trained specifically for this age group, and you stay in the room the whole time.

risk_reversal.headline: If they cling to your leg the whole time? That's fine. That's normal.
risk_reversal.body: The class is free, and there's no contract to think about. If your child has a rough first day, that's completely okay — come back next week, free. Some kids watch for three weeks before they join in. We've seen it a hundred times.

problem.headline: You're not sure they're ready.
checklist: "He won't sit still for five minutes."
"She's shy — she hides behind my leg with new people."
"I'm just not sure he's ready for something like this yet."
problem.closing: For most preschool parents, this would be their child's first structured class of any kind. That nervous feeling is normal — and it's the whole reason this class exists.
